verses.latin_vulgate: Hominis est animam præparare,  et Domini gubernare linguam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְאָדָ֥ם מַעַרְכֵי־לֵ֑ב וּ֝מֵיְהֹוָ֗ה מַעֲנֵ֥ה לָשֽׁוֹן׃
verses.king_james: The preparations of the heart in man, and the answer of the tongue, is from the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes viæ hominis patent oculis ejus ;  spirituum ponderator est Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּֽל־דַּרְכֵי־אִ֭ישׁ זַ֣ךְ בְּעֵינָ֑יו וְתֹכֵ֖ן רוּח֣וֹת יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but the Lord weigheth the spirits.

verses.latin_vulgate: Revela Domino opera tua,  et dirigentur cogitationes tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גֹּ֣ל אֶל־יְהֹוָ֣ה מַעֲשֶׂ֑יךָ וְ֝יִכֹּ֗נוּ מַחְשְׁבֹתֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Commit thy works unto the Lord, and thy thoughts shall be established.

verses.latin_vulgate: Universa propter semetipsum operatus est Dominus ;  impium quoque ad diem malum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֤ל פָּעַ֣ל יְ֭הֹוָה לַֽמַּעֲנֵ֑הוּ וְגַם־רָ֝שָׁ֗ע לְי֣וֹם רָעָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The Lord hath made all things for himself: yea, even the wicked for the day of evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abominatio Domini est omnis arrogans ;  etiamsi manus ad manum fuerit, non est innocens.  Initium viæ bonæ facere justitiam ;  accepta est autem apud Deum magis quam immolare hostias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה כׇּל־גְּבַהּ־לֵ֑ב יָ֥ד לְ֝יָ֗ד לֹ֣א יִנָּקֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: Every one that is proud in heart is an abomination to the Lord: though hand join in hand, he shall not be unpunished.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misericordia et veritate redimitur iniquitas,  et in timore Domini declinatur a malo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּחֶ֣סֶד וֶ֭אֱמֶת יְכֻפַּ֣ר עָוֺ֑ן וּבְיִרְאַ֥ת יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה ס֣וּר מֵרָֽע׃
verses.king_james: By mercy and truth iniquity is purged: and by the fear of the Lord men depart from evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum placuerint Domino viæ hominis,  inimicos quoque ejus convertet ad pacem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּרְצ֣וֹת יְ֭הֹוָה דַּרְכֵי־אִ֑ישׁ גַּם־א֝וֹיְבָ֗יו יַשְׁלִ֥ם אִתּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: When a man’s ways please the Lord, he maketh even his enemies to be at peace with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est parum cum justitia  quam multi fructus cum iniquitate.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טוֹב־מְ֭עַט בִּצְדָקָ֑ה מֵרֹ֥ב תְּ֝בוּא֗וֹת בְּלֹ֣א מִשְׁפָּֽט׃
verses.king_james: Better is a little with righteousness than great revenues without right.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cor hominis disponit viam suam,  sed Domini est dirigere gressus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֣ב אָ֭דָם יְחַשֵּׁ֣ב דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וַ֝יהֹוָ֗ה יָכִ֥ין צַעֲדֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: A man’s heart deviseth his way: but the Lord directeth his steps.

verses.latin_vulgate: Divinatio in labiis regis ;  in judicio non errabit os ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קֶ֤סֶם ׀ עַֽל־שִׂפְתֵי־מֶ֑לֶךְ בְּ֝מִשְׁפָּ֗ט לֹ֣א יִמְעַל־פִּֽיו׃
verses.king_james: A divine sentence is in the lips of the king: his mouth transgresseth not in judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pondus et statera judicia Domini sunt,  et opera ejus omnes lapides sacculi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֶּ֤לֶס ׀ וּמֹאזְנֵ֣י מִ֭שְׁפָּט לַיהֹוָ֑ה מַ֝עֲשֵׂ֗הוּ כׇּל־אַבְנֵי־כִֽיס׃
verses.king_james: A just weight and balance are the Lord’s: all the weights of the bag are his work.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abominabiles regi qui agunt impie,  quoniam justitia firmatur solium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת מְ֭לָכִים עֲשׂ֣וֹת רֶ֑שַׁע כִּ֥י בִ֝צְדָקָ֗ה יִכּ֥וֹן כִּסֵּֽא׃
verses.king_james: It is an abomination to kings to commit wickedness: for the throne is established by righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Voluntas regum labia justa ;  qui recta loquitur diligetur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רְצ֣וֹן מְ֭לָכִים שִׂפְתֵי־צֶ֑דֶק וְדֹבֵ֖ר יְשָׁרִ֣ים יֶאֱהָֽב׃
verses.king_james: Righteous lips are the delight of kings; and they love him that speaketh right.

verses.latin_vulgate: Indignatio regis nuntii mortis,  et vir sapiens placabit eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֲמַת־מֶ֥לֶךְ מַלְאֲכֵי־מָ֑וֶת וְאִ֖ישׁ חָכָ֣ם יְכַפְּרֶֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: The wrath of a king is as messengers of death: but a wise man will pacify it.

verses.latin_vulgate: In hilaritate vultus regis vita,  et clementia ejus quasi imber serotinus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאוֹר־פְּנֵי־מֶ֥לֶךְ חַיִּ֑ים וּ֝רְצוֹנ֗וֹ כְּעָ֣ב מַלְקֽוֹשׁ׃
verses.king_james: In the light of the king’s countenance is life; and his favour is as a cloud of the latter rain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posside sapientiam, quia auro melior est,  et acquire prudentiam, quia pretiosior est argento.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קְֽנֹה־חׇכְמָ֗ה מַה־טּ֥וֹב מֵחָר֑וּץ וּקְנ֥וֹת בִּ֝ינָ֗ה נִבְחָ֥ר מִכָּֽסֶף׃
verses.king_james: How much better is it to get wisdom than gold! and to get understanding rather to be chosen than silver!

verses.latin_vulgate: Semita justorum declinat mala ;  custos animæ suæ servat viam suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְסִלַּ֣ת יְ֭שָׁרִים ס֣וּר מֵרָ֑ע שֹׁמֵ֥ר נַ֝פְשׁ֗וֹ נֹצֵ֥ר דַּרְכּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil: he that keepeth his way preserveth his soul.

verses.latin_vulgate: Contritionem præcedit superbia,  et ante ruinam exaltatur spiritus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִפְנֵי־שֶׁ֥בֶר גָּא֑וֹן וְלִפְנֵ֥י כִ֝שָּׁל֗וֹן גֹּ֣בַהּ רֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: Pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est humiliari cum mitibus  quam dividere spolia cum superbis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֣וֹב שְׁפַל־ר֭וּחַ אֶת־[עֲנָוִ֑ים] (עניים) מֵחַלֵּ֥ק שָׁ֝לָ֗ל אֶת־גֵּאִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Better it is to be of an humble spirit with the lowly, than to divide the spoil with the proud.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eruditus in verbo reperiet bona,  et qui sperat in Domino beatus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַשְׂכִּ֣יל עַל־דָּ֭בָר יִמְצָא־ט֑וֹב וּבוֹטֵ֖חַ בַּיהֹוָ֣ה אַשְׁרָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: He that handleth a matter wisely shall find good: and whoso trusteth in the Lord, happy is he.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui sapiens est corde appellabitur prudens,  et qui dulcis eloquio majora percipiet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַֽחֲכַם־לֵ֭ב יִקָּרֵ֣א נָב֑וֹן וּמֶ֥תֶק שְׂ֝פָתַ֗יִם יֹסִ֥יף לֶֽקַח׃
verses.king_james: The wise in heart shall be called prudent: and the sweetness of the lips increaseth learning.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fons vitæ eruditio possidentis ;  doctrina stultorum fatuitas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְק֣וֹר חַ֭יִּים שֵׂ֣כֶל בְּעָלָ֑יו וּמוּסַ֖ר אֱוִלִ֣ים אִוֶּֽלֶת׃
verses.king_james: Understanding is a wellspring of life unto him that hath it: but the instruction of fools is folly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cor sapientis erudiet os ejus,  et labiis ejus addet gratiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֣ב חָ֭כָם יַשְׂכִּ֣יל פִּ֑יהוּ וְעַל־שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו יֹסִ֥יף לֶֽקַח׃
verses.king_james: The heart of the wise teacheth his mouth, and addeth learning to his lips.

verses.latin_vulgate: Favus mellis composita verba ;  dulcedo animæ sanitas ossium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צוּף־דְּ֭בַשׁ אִמְרֵי־נֹ֑עַם מָת֥וֹק לַ֝נֶּ֗פֶשׁ וּמַרְפֵּ֥א לָעָֽצֶם׃
verses.king_james: Pleasant words are as an honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.

verses.latin_vulgate: Est via quæ videtur homini recta,  et novissima ejus ducunt ad mortem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵ֤שׁ דֶּ֣רֶךְ יָ֭שָׁר לִפְנֵי־אִ֑ישׁ וְ֝אַחֲרִיתָ֗הּ דַּרְכֵי־מָֽוֶת׃
verses.king_james: There is a way that seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Anima laborantis laborat sibi,  quia compulit eum os suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֶ֣פֶשׁ עָ֭מֵל עָ֣מְלָה לּ֑וֹ כִּֽי־אָכַ֖ף עָלָ֣יו פִּֽיהוּ׃
verses.king_james: He that laboureth laboureth for himself; for his mouth craveth it of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir impius fodit malum,  et in labiis ejus ignis ardescit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֣ישׁ בְּ֭לִיַּעַל כֹּרֶ֣ה רָעָ֑ה וְעַל־[שְׂ֝פָת֗וֹ] (שפתיו) כְּאֵ֣שׁ צָרָֽבֶת׃
verses.king_james: An ungodly man diggeth up evil: and in his lips there is as a burning fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Homo perversus suscitat lites,  et verbosus separat principes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֣ישׁ תַּ֭הְפֻּכוֹת יְשַׁלַּ֣ח מָד֑וֹן וְ֝נִרְגָּ֗ן מַפְרִ֥יד אַלּֽוּף׃
verses.king_james: A froward man soweth strife: and a whisperer separateth chief friends.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir iniquus lactat amicum suum,  et ducit eum per viam non bonam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֣ישׁ חָ֭מָס יְפַתֶּ֣ה רֵעֵ֑הוּ וְ֝הוֹלִיכ֗וֹ בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ לֹא־טֽוֹב׃
verses.king_james: A violent man enticeth his neighbour, and leadeth him into the way that is not good.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui attonitis oculis cogitat prava,  mordens labia sua perficit malum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֹצֶ֣ה עֵ֭ינָיו לַחְשֹׁ֣ב תַּהְפֻּכ֑וֹת קֹרֵ֥ץ שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו כִּלָּ֥ה רָעָֽה׃
verses.king_james: He shutteth his eyes to devise froward things: moving his lips he bringeth evil to pass.

verses.latin_vulgate: Corona dignitatis senectus,  quæ in viis justitiæ reperietur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲטֶ֣רֶת תִּפְאֶ֣רֶת שֵׂיבָ֑ה בְּדֶ֥רֶךְ צְ֝דָקָ֗ה תִּמָּצֵֽא׃
verses.king_james: The hoary head is a crown of glory, if it be found in the way of righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melior est patiens viro forti,  et qui dominatur animo suo expugnatore urbium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֤וֹב אֶ֣רֶךְ אַ֭פַּיִם מִגִּבּ֑וֹר וּמֹשֵׁ֥ל בְּ֝רוּח֗וֹ מִלֹּכֵ֥ד עִֽיר׃
verses.king_james: He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty; and he that ruleth his spirit than he that taketh a city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sortes mittuntur in sinum,  sed a Domino temperantur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּ֭חֵיק יוּטַ֣ל אֶת־הַגּוֹרָ֑ל וּ֝מֵיְהֹוָ֗ה כׇּל־מִשְׁפָּטֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The lot is cast into the lap; but the whole disposing thereof is of the Lord.
